summary:
Gestational age is strongly linked to birth weight, with shorter durations leading to lower birth weights; therefore, misclassification of gestational age—such as through inaccurate dating methods—could distort apparent racial differences in birth weight, potentially explaining some of the disparity observed between Aboriginal Australians and White individuals. This suggests that measurement error rather than biological factors may underlie part of the observed variation.  

(Word count = 68)
